cluster_note: The run metadata names exactid, but the locked Stage -1 proposal and all parsed conjectures define a PartialID/support-function structural time-series problem; this setup preserves the locked proposal parameters.

substrate_audit.method: declaration-search audit only; no source files opened or cited
substrate_audit.result: No reusable declarations surfaced at the project root for these ExactID names.
substrate_audit.bypass_justified: The artifact is algebraic over observable VAR support-function objects rather than an SCM adjustment theorem.

locked_parameters.anchor_cluster: PartialID / support-function structural time-series identification with partial sign-zero restrictions and higher-cumulant contact restrictions defining the sharp response set
locked_parameters.observable_distribution_P: the population law of a covariance-stationary finite-order VAR(p) process Y_t, summarized by reduced-form response rows Gamma_h = E[Y_{t+h} u_t'] Sigma_u^{-1} for h=0,...,H, innovation covariance Sigma_u, and fourth cumulant tensor K_u = cum4(u_t)
locked_parameters.parameter_theta: the structural impulse-response vector psi_j(Q)=(e_y' Gamma_h b_j(Q))_{h=0}^H and its scalar report theta_w(Q)=w' psi_j(Q)
locked_parameters.identifying_restrictions: finite-order LP/VAR population rows; unit-variance shocks; partial sign-zero restrictions A psi_j(Q) >= 0 and Z psi_j(Q)=0; a fourth-cumulant structural representation whose off-diagonal co-kurtosis leakage is bounded by eta >= 0; target cumulant separation delta_4 > 0; and the common-contact requirement that the same rotation generating an LP response lies inside the eta-leakage cumulant contact set
locked_parameters.upgrade_axis: generalization of the parent v1 independent-shock cumulant diagonalization premise from eta=0 to eta-bounded dependent/non-diagonal fourth-cumulant leakage, with v1 recovered as the eta=0 corner
locked_parameters.operational_quantity: eta_w^*(P), the critical leakage radius at which a support-exposed LP/sign maximizer first enters the higher-cumulant SVAR contact set; strict non-equivalence is governed by eta < eta_w^*(P)
locked_parameters.bounding_functional: support functions h_LP(w;P) and h_eta(w;P) over, respectively, the LP/sign relaxation and the eta-leakage higher-cumulant contact set; the non-equivalence gap is Delta_w^eta(P)=h_LP(w;P)-h_eta(w;P)

named_assumptions.label: ass:finite-var
named_assumptions.statement: The population law \(P\) is generated by a covariance-stationary finite-order VAR(p) with innovation \(u_t\), \(\Sigma_u\succ0\), finite fourth moments, and response rows \(\Gamma_h\) equal to the unrestricted population VAR response rows for \(h=0,\ldots,H\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:normalization
named_assumptions.statement: Structural shocks have identity covariance and each impact matrix is \(B(Q)=\Sigma_u^{1/2}Q\) for \(Q\in O(n)\).  The shock label \(j\) is fixed after the usual sign and permutation normalization.

named_assumptions.label: ass:sign-zero
named_assumptions.statement: The matrices \(A\in\R^{m_A\times(H+1)}\) and \(Z\in\R^{m_Z\times(H+1)}\) are known, and \(\mathcal Q_{\mathrm{LP}}(P)=\{Q:A\psi_j(Q)\ge0,\ Z\psi_j(Q)=0\}\) is nonempty and compact as a closed subset of \(O(n)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:cumulants
named_assumptions.statement: The fourth cumulant tensor \(K_u=\operatorname{cum}_4(u_t)\) is observed at the population level.  For each \(Q\), \(D(Q,\kappa)=\sum_k\kappa_kb_k(Q)^{\otimes4}\) is the pure diagonal fourth-cumulant component in the \(B(Q)\) basis.

named_assumptions.label: ass:separated-target
named_assumptions.statement: The admissible pure diagonal cumulants satisfy \(\kappa\in\mathcal K_{\delta_4}(j)\) for a fixed \(\delta_4>0\).  Gaussian and equal-target-kurtosis boundaries are excluded from strict-gap statements unless explicitly handled as limiting cases.

named_assumptions.label: ass:leakage-class
named_assumptions.statement: For every \(Q\), \(\mathcal L_\eta(Q)\) is the closed convex set of symmetric fourth-order tensors whose coordinates in the \(B(Q)\) structural basis have zero pure diagonal terms and Euclidean off-diagonal coefficient norm at most \(\eta\), using the coordinate convention \(r_4(Q,\kappa;P)\) from the formal setup.  The sets are nested in \(\eta\), contain only \(0\) when \(\eta=0\), and vary continuously with \(Q\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:contact-nonempty
named_assumptions.statement: For every leakage radius \(\eta\) appearing in a finite support comparison, \(\mathcal Q_\eta(P)\) is nonempty.  Empty-contact cases are interpreted as failed higher-cumulant SVAR feasibility rather than finite LP/SVAR disagreement.

named_assumptions.label: ass:generic-exposed
named_assumptions.statement: The report direction \(w\in\R^{H+1}\) exposes a nonempty compact LP maximizer set \(\mathcal M_w(P)\), and \(\theta_w(Q)\) is not constant on any positive-dimensional feasible component that changes the leakage-adjusted mismatch \(m_\eta(Q;P)\).

named_assumptions.label: ass:regular-frontier
named_assumptions.statement: Near the boundary \(\eta=\eta_w^*(P)\), the zero set \(m_\eta(Q;P)=0\) intersects the exposed LP support face transversally, except on a lower-dimensional algebraic exceptional set.  This is the regularity condition whose necessity or sufficiency is part of Conjecture 1.

named_assumptions.label: ass:two-shock
named_assumptions.statement: For the closed-form witness, \(n=2\), \(p=1\), \(H\ge1\), both rotation and reflection components of \(O(2)\) are included, the sign-zero restrictions leave a nondegenerate angular interval on at least one component, and the target and non-target pure cumulants are separated by \(\delta_4\).

estimand_functional: h_{\mathrm{LP}}(w;P)=\sup_{Q\in\mathcal Q_{\mathrm{LP}}(P)}\theta_w(Q),\quad h_\eta(w;P)=\sup_{Q\in\mathcal Q_\eta(P)}\theta_w(Q),\quad \eta_w^*(P)=\inf\{\eta'\ge0:\mathcal M_w(P)\cap\mathcal Q_{\eta'}(P)\ne\emptyset\}

conjectures.label: critical-leakage-frontier
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~ef{ass:finite-var}--ef{ass:regular-frontier}, fix a population law \(P\), report direction \(w\), and leakage radius \(\eta\) with \(\mathcal Q_\eta(P)
e\emptyset\).  The support-function equality frontier is sharp:
\[
h_{\mathrm{LP}}(w;P)=h_\eta(w;P)
\quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad
\eta\ge \eta_w^*(P),
\]
where
\[
\eta_w^*(P)=
\inf\{\eta'\ge0:\mathcal M_w(P)\cap\mathcal Q_{\eta'}(P)
e\emptyset\}.
\]
Consequently, for every \(\eta<\eta_w^*(P)\),
\[
\Delta_w^\eta(P)=h_{\mathrm{LP}}(w;P)-h_\eta(w;P)>0,
\]
while for every \(\eta\ge\eta_w^*(P)\) the exposed LP support value is attainable by a rotation whose fourth cumulants match \(K_u\) up to allowed co-kurtosis leakage.  On a nonempty open semi-algebraic class of observable objects, \(\eta_w^*(P)\) is positive and finite, so bounded dependence creates a genuine phase transition between parent-style non-equivalence and restored LP/SVAR equivalence.

conjectures.label: two-shock-formula
conjectures.statement:
Under Assumptions~ef{ass:finite-var}--ef{ass:generic-exposed} and ef{ass:two-shock}, parameterize the two components of \(O(2)\) by rotation/reflection angles \(\varphi\).  Let the LP/sign feasible set on a component be \(I_{\mathrm{LP}}\), and write the report objective as
\[
	heta_w(\varphi)=a_w\cos\varphi+b_w\sin\varphi=ho_w\cos(\varphi-\varphi_w).
\]
Let \(r_4(\varphi,\kappa;P)\) be the vector of off-diagonal fourth-cumulant residual coordinates in the structural basis and \(d_4(\varphi,\kappa;P)\) the pure-diagonal residual.  If no \(\kappa\in\mathcal K_{\delta_4}(j)\) satisfies \(d_4(\varphi,\kappa;P)=0\) at any LP support angle on the component, set the component value of \(\eta_w^*(P)\) to \(+\infty\).  Otherwise the critical leakage radius on that component is
\[
\eta_w^*(P)=
\min_{\substack{\varphi\in\arg\max_{\phi\in I_{\mathrm{LP}}}	heta_w(\phi)\
\kappa\in\mathcal K_{\delta_4}(j),\ d_4(\varphi,\kappa;P)=0}}
\|r_4(\varphi,\kappa;P)\|_{\mathrm{off}},
\]
with the global \(O(2)\) value obtained by taking the minimum over rotation and reflection components.  In the parent corner \(\eta=0\), this reduces to the parent two-shock witness: equality holds only when an LP support angle is one of the cumulant-contact angles; otherwise the support gap is at least \(ho_w(1-\cos d_w)\) for the distance \(d_w\) to the nearest contact angle.

supporting_theorems.label: parent-corner
supporting_theorems.statement:
Under Assumptions~ef{ass:finite-var}--ef{ass:leakage-class}, the contact sets are nested:
\[
\eta_1\le\eta_2\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\mathcal Q_{\eta_1}(P)\subseteq \mathcal Q_{\eta_2}(P)\subseteq\mathcal Q_{\mathrm{LP}}(P),
\]
and therefore \(h_{\eta_1}(w;P)\le h_{\eta_2}(w;P)\le h_{\mathrm{LP}}(w;P)\) for every \(w\).  When \(\eta=0\), \(\mathcal L_0(Q)=\{0\}\) and \(\mathcal Q_0(P)\) is exactly the parent v1 independent-shock cumulant-diagonalizing SVAR/sign set.  If the LP side is restricted to the same \(\eta\)-contact set, the LP and \(\eta\)-SVAR support functions are equal for every \(w\).
